Since the props map 1:1 with the same names, the main concern is whether the code uses `ChartDonutProps` as a type reference.

**Fix 2**: `ChartLabelProps` interface was removed, same situation - replacement is `ChartLabelProps` with same props. The component `ChartLabel` is imported from `@patternfly/react-charts`.

**Fix 2 (ChartLabelProps removed):** Same situation - `ChartLabel` is imported and used as a JSX component (line 31), but `ChartLabelProps` is never used as a type reference. The component itself is still available, and all the props used (`inline`, `style`, `text`) are still present in the new interface. No changes needed.

Both fixes target the removal of the *Props interfaces*, but this file only imports and uses the *components* themselves (`ChartDonut`, `ChartLabel`, `ChartLegend`), not the Props type interfaces. The props used on `ChartDonut` (constrainToVisibleArea, style, height, width, padding, data, events, labels, legendData, legendComponent, legendOrientation, title, subTitle, sortOrder, x, y) and `ChartLabel` (inline, style, text) all map 1:1 to the new interface. No code changes are required.
